feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP CHOTA TALKS Q1 - HYDERABAD - JUST THE BEGINNING!!
entry.content:
On February 22, 2025, the School of Management Studies at the University of Hyderabad hosted an outstanding SAP Chota Talks event. The gathering was a platform for enriching knowledge sharing, fostering community interaction, and sparking engaging discussions about the ever-changing SAP ecosystem.

The event featured insightful speaker sessions, an engaging panel discussion, and a deep dive into the impact of AI within the SAP ecosystem. These sessions provided valuable perspectives on how AI is shaping the future of SAP, sparking meaningful conversations about its potential and challenges.

It served as a wonderful platform for SAP professionals to meet, share experiences, and forge new connections. The event created a dynamic environment for networking, allowing attendees to engage with peers, exchange ideas, and build relationships that will support their growth.

@MaheshCV @Ruthvik_Chowdary Heartfelt thank you for organizing the event. Blog SAP CHOTA TALKS Hyderabad 2025 (Q1) - Event Highlights provides a thorough overview of the sessions, panel discussions, and the valuable knowledge shared during the event, offering a great resource for all those who attended and even those who couldn’t make it. We are excited and eager to see many more events like SAP Chota Talks, where knowledge is shared, connections are made, and innovation thrives.

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP CPI: A Beginner's Handbook || Part 2
entry.content:
Contents Overview

  • JSON to XML Converter
  • XML to JSON Converter
  • Request Reply
  • Send Pallet
  • Message Mapping
  • Value Mapping
  • Splitter
  • XSD File
  • OData

JSON to XML Converter:

Why we add root element?

It acts as the top-level container for all other elements defining the overall structure of the document and ensuring that there is only one parent element for every other element within the xml file.

Why we use namespace mapping?

To prevent naming conflicts between element and attributes from different sources by associating them with unique identifiers.

 

XML to JSON Converter:

Suppress Json root element: we can check this option when we want Json response without root element.

Streaming: To convert a single-line item into an array form.

Request Reply: To allows a user to call an external system and receive a response.

Send Pallet: Using send pallet for sending mail. It acts like request reply.

 

Message Mapping:

A process that transforms data between systems by mapping fields between messages.

In simple words use message mapping for converting one structure to another structure.

Why isn’t message mapping working without root?

Because it is not receiving the header node.

 

Value Mapping:

Value mapping is used to translate source values into target values during data integration.

 

Splitter:

A splitter is used to break message into smaller parts that can be processed independently.

General Splitter: Split a message into individual messages, while preserving the context of the root nodes.

Iterating Splitter: Splits a general document into its constituent elements but does not include the enclosing tags in the individual messages.

 

How to Create Service Key?

  1. Go to the instance and subscriptions.
  2. Now here we’ve to create instance.
  3. After Create the instance we’ll create service key.

* In instance we’ve to choose SAP Process Integration Process Service.

 

XSD File:

An "XSD file" stand for "XML Schema Definition" file, which essentially defines the structure and data used types of an XML message used in integration flows, allowing for data validation and ensuring the consistency of data exchanged between different systems the integration process.

An XSD file specifies the element, attributes, and their data types within an XML message, acting like a blueprint for How the data should be organized.

Validation Tool: By using an XSD, CPI can Validate incoming XML message against the defined structure, identifying any errors or in the data.

XSD files are crucial for creating message mappings in CPI. where you transform data from format (e.g. an external system) to another based on the defined structure.

OData: The OData adapter allows you to communicate with an OData protocol. you can use messages in ATOM or JSON format for Communication.

Host: This is the IP address of the server hosting the OData service.

Port: The port number on which the OData service is listening. The default ports are 80 for http and 443 for https.

Path: This is the base path for the service. it identifies the service and OData and specifies the namespace for the  entities exposed by the service.

Sublevel: A Sublevel typically refers to the depth of nested entities or related data that can be expanded in an OData query.

Entity: An "entity" represents a single, structured record of data, like a customer or product.(Set of Records)

 

Proxy type:

Internet: Internet proxy type is Suitable when your OData service is externally hosted or available on the internet. it allows the adapter to communicate with the service over the public network.

On-premise: The on-premise proxy type is suitable for Scenarios where the OData service is hosted within your organization’s network.

 

Metadata Document:

When you access the URL in your step-1, typically the service will respond with metadata about the available entity sets, properties. relationships and operations exposed by the service.

"The metadata document describes the Structure and capabilities of OData service, allowing the clients to understand how to interact with it programmatically.

In summary, when you access the full URL, you'll receive metadata and information about the available resources and operations provided by the OData service.

*.edmx is the extension of metadata.

 

EDMX (Entity Data Model XSD):

EDMX is an XML-based file that encapsulates the metadata of an OData service. it includes information about entities, properties, relationship and other aspects of the services data model.

entry.title: Welcome to the SAP Community Group in Cape Town!
entry.content:
Greetings, SAP Cape Town enthusiasts! We are thrilled to introduce the SAP Community Group in Cape Town, a space dedicated to fostering collaboration, learning, and innovation within the local SAP ecosystem. Whether you're a seasoned SAP professional, an aspiring developer, or a business leader leveraging SAP solutions, this group is for YOU!

Why Join the SAP Community Group?

Cape Town is home to a vibrant tech scene, and SAP plays a crucial role in driving digital transformation across industries. Our community group is designed to:

✅Connect – Network with like-minded professionals, SAP experts, and industry leaders.

✅Learn – Stay up to date with the latest SAP trends, best practices, and innovations.

✅Share – Exchange knowledge, experiences, and solutions to SAP challenges.

✅Grow – Gain insights into career development, certifications, and new opportunities in SAP.

What to Expect

We aim to create an engaging and interactive platform through:

  • Meetups & Events – Attend in-person and virtual sessions with guest speakers, hands-on workshops, and knowledge-sharing meetups.
  • Discussion Forums – Engage in meaningful discussions, ask questions, and find support from the community.
  • Insights & Resources – Access exclusive content, expert insights, and SAP learning resources.

Get Involved!

We invite you to be part of this exciting journey. Whether you want to contribute as a speaker, share insights, or simply connect with fellow SAP professionals, we welcome your participation!

📌Stay tuned for our first event announcement!

Join the SAP Community Group in Cape Town today and be part of a dynamic network that is shaping the future of SAP in South Africa. Let’s collaborate, innovate, and grow together!

🚀Follow us, share your thoughts, and let’s build something amazing!

About the Initiator 

Robert Peter, the founder of the group, is also the Co-Founder of redcup code, a dynamic SAP consultancy firm in Cape Town specializing in SAP customer experience solutions. He firmly believes that collaboration leads to greater success, which inspired him to establish this group in the vibrant city of Cape Town.

entry.title: Extract Properties in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Identifying and tabulating all the properties used in SAP Commerce (formerly known as SAP Hybris) by extension name can be a complex task, as the platform is highly customizable, and the properties can vary widely based on specific configurations, versions, and custom developments. However, here’s a general approach you can take to gather this information:

1. Understand the Structure:

SAP Commerce is structured into a number of extensions. Each extension can have its own set of properties defined in *.properties files. These properties can be found in multiple places:

  • project.properties: Global properties for the entire SAP Commerce project.
  • local.properties: Developer-specific properties, usually for local environment settings.
  • aspect-specific.properties: CCv2 aspect-specific properties, usually used by CI-CD processes and specified in manifest.json file.
  • environment-specific.properties: CCv2 environment-specific properties, usually used by CI-CD processes and specified in manifest.json file.
  • Extension-specific custom properties: Found under resources within each extension (either OOTB or custom).

2. Locate Properties Files:

Properties are typically stored in .properties files in the resources directory of each extension. To gather a comprehensive list:

  • Core Extensions:

    • Check the standard SAP Commerce extensions in the platform directory.

  • Custom Extensions:

    • Review any custom extensions developed specifically for your implementation.

3. Extract Properties:

Once you have located the properties files, you can use scripts to extract and tabulate them. Below is a general approach using basic command-line tools and scripts.

Using Shell Script (Linux/MacOS)

 
#!/bin/bash

# Path to your SAP Commerce extensions directory
EXTENSIONS_PATH="/path/to/your/sap/commerce/extensions"

# Output file
OUTPUT_FILE="properties_table.csv"

# Initialize the output file
echo "Extension Name,Property" > $OUTPUT_FILE

# Loop through each extension directory
for EXT in $(find $EXTENSIONS_PATH -maxdepth 2 -type d)
do
    EXT_NAME=$(basename $EXT)
    # Find all .properties files within the extension
    for PROP_FILE in $(find $EXT -name "*.properties")
    do
        # Extract non-comment lines from properties files
        grep -v '^#' $PROP_FILE | grep -E '^[a-zA-Z]' | while read -r LINE
        do
            # Split the line into property name and value
            PROPERTY_NAME=$(echo $LINE | cut -d'=' -f1)
            # Append to the output CSV
            echo "$EXT_NAME,$PROPERTY_NAME" >> $OUTPUT_FILE
        done
    done
done

echo "Properties have been extracted to $OUTPUT_FILE"

Notes:

  • Adaptation Required: Modify the EXTENSIONS_PATH to point to your specific SAP Commerce extensions directory.
  • Permissions: Ensure you have the correct permissions to read files in your SAP Commerce directory.
  • Custom Environments: If your team has localized additional properties files or locations, include those paths in the script logic.

4. Automation and Maintenance:

  • Regularly update your properties table as configurations change or new extensions are added.
  • Consider integrating this extraction process into your CI/CD pipeline to keep the documentation up-to-date automatically.

By structuring the data into a CSV file, you can easily import it into a spreadsheet application for analysis and management. Remember, this script is a starting point and may need customization based on the specific layout and configuration of your SAP Commerce implementation.

Hope above is helpful to get you started.
Let me know if there are any issues in the script.

entry.title: Fetching PDFs from SAP Using CPI and HTTP Adapter.
entry.content:
In this blog article, we will walk you through the process of fetching PDF from SAP using ODATA URL by giving some input parameters.

Here we are using HTTP Adapter instead of ODATA adapter.

For fetching PDFs from SAP, the HTTP adapter is preferred due to better binary data handling, performance, authentication support, and flexibility, while the OData adapter is better suited for structured data exchange.

  • Here I’m sending some input parameters from postman and then I will store these inputs in exchange property (content modifier) and these stored properties are later used as parameters in the HTTP adapter.     

           

 

  • Adding Request-Reply Step

To consume the OData URL, add a Request-Reply step in your integration flow and connect it with the receiver using the HTTP Adapter.

Now, configure the HTTP Adapter with the necessary details:

Endpoint: Enter the URL in Address.                     

URL Breakdown: -

The following OData URL is used to fetch billing proforma details from the SAP system:

http://Host:Port/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCONTRACT_SRV/BILLING_PROFORMASet(FROM_DT='${property.FromDate}',TO_DT='${property.ToDate}',VBELN='${property.Vbeln}')/$value

                     Components of the URL

  • Base URL: http://host:Port /sap/opu/odata/sap/
    • This is the entry point of the SAP OData service.
  • Service Name: ZCONTRACT_SRV
    • This is the custom OData service created in SAP.
  • Entity Set: BILLING_PROFORMASet
    • Represents the collection of billing proforma data.
  • Query Parameters:
    • FROM_DT='${property.FromDate}' → Start date for filtering the billing proforma.
    • TO_DT='${property.ToDate}' → End date for filtering the billing proforma.
    • VBELN='${property.Vbeln}' → Sales document number (optional for specific filtering).
  • $value:
    • This keyword is used to fetch the raw value of the requested data, typically used for downloading files or direct content retrieval.

2.Select the proxy type: 

  • On-Premise: The on-Premise proxy type is suitable for scenarios where the OData service is hosted within your organization's network. It allows the adapter to establish a connection within the internal network, ensuring secure and controlled access.

3.Select the Method:

  • Here we will choose Get method because we are fetching data from SAP.

4.Authentication:

  • Specify the authentication here we are using Basic Auth and provide the required credentials.

 Leave the remaining fields as empty as they are not mandatory.

5.Now save and deploy the I-Flow

Testing Using Postman

Trigger the endpoint from Postman below are the screenshot of the response.

You can convert the retrieved PDF to a Base64 string, use the Base64 Converter pallets in CPI.

entry.title: Send Attachment Using SFTP in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Software Needed

  1. SFTP server details if you have, else we will create a SFTP free server.
  2. FileZilla installed in your system to check the server files.

Steps:

  1. Go to https://sftpcloud.io/tools/free-sftp-server website & create a free SFTP server

2. Create a new instance.

3. Enter a Name & Storage → Click Create.

5. Now install FileZilla on your system.

  • Enter Host Name, Username, Password, Port No and click Quick Connect.

  • After clicking Quick Connect, verify the connection status.

6. Open Tenant

 In SAP CPI: Monitor ---> Overview ---> Test Connectivity

     Copy the host key, save it in Notepad.

In SAP CPI: Monitor ---> Overview ---> Manage Security Material ---> Security Material

Now Upload Your Host Key (that you saved in notepad)

 

Create & Configure User Credentials

Monitor → User Credentials → Create New

Enter Username & Password → Save.

7.Create Integration Artifact to Pull Attachments from SFTP

 

8.  Configure SFTP

 Add the directory path that you created in FileZilla

Address :  Host Name

Credentials : In Monitor à User Credentials (that you created above)

9. Configure Mail

In Address : smtp.gmail.com:465

Protection Type : SMTPS

Then in processing we have Body Mime-Type

  • This defines the format of the email body.
  • Available options:
    • Application/JSON → Sends email body as JSON.
    • Application/Octet-Stream → Sends as raw binary data.
    • Application/X-GZip → Sends the body as a compressed GZIP file.
    • Application/XML → Sends data in XML format.
    • Application/ZIP → Sends the body as a ZIP file.
    • Text/CSV → Sends the body as a CSV file.
    • Text/HTML → Formats the email in HTML.
    • Text/Plain → Sends the email as plain text (default).

10. Testing in Postman

 

Used Postman to test an SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) API endpoint. The request was made using the GET method to the following URL:

 

Endpoint URL:  <--- //2193trail.it-cpitrial03-rt.cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com/http/xml/test ---> 

 

 

After sending the request, I received a 200 OK response, which indicates a successful connection.

Conclusion

This guide covered the complete process of setting up an SFTP server and configuring FileZilla for secure file transfers. It also detailed the integration of SFTP with SAP CPI to enable seamless connectivity. Additionally, the steps for configuring SMTP to send email attachments securely were explained. Finally, the setup was tested using Postman, ensuring a successful connection with a 200 OK response. By following these steps, you can efficiently automate file transfers in SAP CPI and streamline email delivery using SFTP.

Thank You,

Pranav Sharma

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP CPI: A Beginner's Handbook || Part 3
entry.content:
Contents Overview

  • XSLT Mapping
  • JSON / JSON Object / JSON Arrays
  • XML / XML Schema
  • XML Attribute / XML Element
  • Filter
  • Router
  • Groovy
  • Write Varibale 
  • Multicast
  • Gather and Join

 

XSLT Mapping: "Extensible Stylesheet Language Transformations" mapping refers to the process of using XSLT to transform and map data between different formats during integration scenarios.

XSLT is a language used to transform XML structure into various format.

XSLT mapping is a powerful tool in SAP CPI that enables you to manage complex data transformations and mapping in integration scenarios, easing seamless communication between different systems with varying data formats.

JSON: JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) is a standard text-based format for representing structured data based on JavaScript object syntax.

JSON Object:

  • JSON object literals are surrounded by curly braces.
  • JSON object literals contain Key/Value pairs.
  • Keys and Values are separated by a colon.

JSON Arrays: JSON arrays values must be type string, number object, array, boolean or null.

XML: XML (Extensible Markup Language) is a way to store and exchange data between systems. It's a file format that can be read by both humans and computers. 

XML Schema: An XML schema describes the structure of an XML document.

XML Attributes: Attributes values must always be quoted. for a person's gender, the <person> element can be written like this-

XML Element: An XML the element is everything from element's start tag to the elements end tag.

Filter: Use filter to from an extract information from an incoming message, in other words you Filter out parts of the message that you don’t want and extract only the data that you want.

Router:  We use router when you specify conditions based on which the messages are routed to receive an interface during runtime.

The router is a tool directs messages to different recipient’s based on specific conditions.

Groovy: Groovy is an object-oriented language which is based on java platform.

Import: The import statement can be used to import the functionality of other libraries. which can be used in your code. By default, groovy includes the following libraries in your code

import java.lang.*

import java.util.*

import java.iot.*

import java.net.*

import groovy. lang.*

import groovy.util.*

import java. math. BigInteger

import java. math. BigDecimal

// using a println statement to print output to the console.

Println(“Hello World!”);

*Identifiers: Identifiers are used to define variables, functions or other user defined variables,  identifiers start with letter, dollar or an underscore. they cannot start with number.

// use semi-colon after statement.

def x = 5;

define the processData(Message, message)

It is a function that runs the code inside it when the system calls this script.

In processData (Message, message), the word Message and message have different purpose:

1) Message (with a capital M) is the type (or class) of the object. it tells the system what kind of working with.

2) message (with a lowercase m) is the name of the variable that holds the actual data or object passed into the function.

Example-

  • processData is the recipe function
  • Message (this is a recipe for cake)
  • message (the cake is named Chocolate cake)

Write Variable: Write variables to share data across different integration flows (deployed on the same tenant).

A "Write Variable" step is used to store a piece of data (like a timestamp, token, or configuration value) as a variable that can be accessed and reused across different parts of an integration flow or even in other integration flows within the same tenant.

Multicast: We can use the Multicast step to send copies of the same message to multiple routes. we can send copies to all routes at once using Parallel Multicast or in an order of your choice using Sequential Multicast. This allows you to perform multiple operations on the same message in a single integration process. Without Multicast, you needed multiple integration processes to perform this task.

Gather And Join: The Gather step merges messages from different routes (into a single message) with the option to define certain strategies how to combine the initial messages. The Join step is used in combination with the Gather step. It brings together the messages from different routes, but it does not affect the content of the messages.

Difference between Gather and Join: The Gather step merges messages from different routes into one, while the Join step combines messages from different routes without changing their content. 

Example Gather:

Input:

Message 1: {"OrderID": "123", "Status": "Shipped"}

Message 2: {"OrderID": "123", "DeliveryDate": "2025-02-01"}

Output (After Gather):

{"OrderID": "123", "Status": "Shipped”, "DeliveryDate": "2025-02-01"}

 

Example Join:

Input:

Message 1: {"OrderID": "123", "Status": "Shipped"}

Message 2: {"OrderID": "123", "DeliveryDate": "2025-02-01"}

Output (After Join):

[  {"OrderID": "123", "Status": "Shipped"},  {"OrderID": "123", "DeliveryDate": "2025-02-01"}]

entry.title: Sydney Stammtisch Recap 2502
entry.content:
The Shelbourne Hotel Rooftop was another new venue for #SAPStammtischSYD and we had big crowd come to check it out. Thanks to everyone who came!

It was a sunny day but very humid (as it usually is in Sydney in February). At first I was disappointed that our tables weren't directly under the retractable roof, but then I realised that our space was air conditioned and much more clement.

I couldn't really count numbers precisely but I would estimate we had a record crowd, probably over 40 people.

I'm thinking we will need to plan further ahead for future events, as we really need a large space reserved for us.  Most likely the next event will be on a Thursday in May.

Perhaps we should return to The Orient Hotel? Thursday seems to be the most popular night...would you agree?  Or is there a new venue we should try?  Let us know in the comments below

Thanks to @jeff_t for the last 6 photos below. Feel free to add photos in the comments

entry.title: SAP BTP应用开发扩展线上课程
entry.content:
由SAP Adoption Service Center大神带领大家每天四小时的实战课程

注意要自备环境：Cloud Foundry Runtime, SAP Business Application Studio 或者 SAP Build Code, SAP HANA Cloud 或者 PostgreSQL on SAP BP - hyperscaler option, SAP Build Work Zone - standard edition)

entry.title: From Tech Talk to Sweet Moments "A Memorable Evening in New York"
entry.content:
As February comes to an end, I had the pleasure of meeting two knowledgeable individuals, @Suresh_Sadhu  and @smarchesini . Our conversation centered around key topics in SAP, particularly sharing individual experiences of day-to-day work schedules and the latest updates from the SAP community meetup. It was an insightful exchange of ideas, and we left the conversation feeling inspired and more informed.

Beyond the work talk, we also shared some personal interests. It was interesting to hear about what drives each of us outside of the professional world, whether it’s exploring new hobbies, reading, or simply taking time to relax. These personal insights added a refreshing perspective to the conversation.

Afterward, we took a walk through the lively streets of Times Square, New York. The energy, bright lights, and constant activity made it a unique experience. It was a great way to unwind and reflect on the discussions we had.

To end the evening, we visited a familiar restaurant and bakery and enjoyed their famous cheesecake. It was the perfect treat to finish off a rewarding day, surrounded by good food and great company.

Overall, it was a fantastic evening filled with valuable conversations and moments of relaxation. I’m looking forward to more enriching experiences like this in the future!

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Sandra Solís
entry.content:
Hi friends!! I am honored to start a new SAP Community series "Blog It Forward" as an exciting blogging challenge.

What can you expect? 
One blog will start a journey where you'll get to know other community members like never before. We will read about their dreams, ambitions and what fascinates them in the world of SAP. So, let’s get started and meet each other.

What can you do? 
Like and comment on your fellow community members blogs—exchange ideas, share your common interests, and have FUN!

I am Sandra Solís, an ABAP developer from Mexico.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

My expertise is in ABAP, SAP HANA and ABAP Cloud. When I was a kid, I knew computers and my curiosity to know how they work made me decide at 10 years old that I wanted to become a developer or someone working with computers. So, from high school to university, I focused entirely on learning about computers and everything related to them, especially programming. I was introduced to SAP 11 years ago, when I was looking for a job after I graduated as a Systems Engineer, a friend invited me to work with him on an SAP consultant enterprise. I did interviews and I got the job as an ABAP developer even though I didn’t know anything about it! But they trained me and that's how I became an ABAP developer. I got to know this world of SAP and I loved it. 

However, my journey wasn’t always easy, when my boss asked me to get certified I got a huge surprise when I failed the exam, not once, not twice, but 3 times! A few years later, I decided to try to get certified again, but I made some changes. First I started looking for resources and I found the SAP Community and SAP Learning. 

Second I started to draw all I read and learned, and combined with my passion for my job, I created my YouTube channel called “Mundo Abaper” (Abaper world) to share in Spanish what I was studying with visuals to people who learn visually like me who had failed their exams. When I realized people started following me, I started to create videos teaching ABAP and some tips and hacks I have learned. Thanks to this, I found my own community which I call #ABAPlovers and every day I make my videos thinking of how I can improve myself, share better content and help the ABAP community.

BTW, if you knew my YT channel and you are an #ABAPlover❤️, let me know in the comments👇😉 

A fun fact about me is that I love painting and drawing. Painting helps me stop thinking and enjoy a relaxing moment. I can paint from simple things and landscapes to portraits of people. I have a tiny sketchbook where I paint the places I have visited.

 On a fun day I painted the portraits of some SAP Developer Advocates with our favorite drink, Coffee. Yes! Coffee can also be used to paint. I delivered the paintings  to them in person and they loved it. 

I have two pet turtles, they are so big, cute and friendly, they even give me their paws.

Additionally, I love everything related to horror content like books, movies, series or real stories.  I enjoy exercising, especially dance and boxing classes. Creating content for my YouTube channel and engaging with the SAP Community are also a big part of my life and I follow a dedicated skincare and anti-aging routine as part of my commitment to self-care and well-being.

Another fun fact about me is that I won Devtoberfest 2 times! Thanks to this, the first time I went to Hana House, LA and I met advocates Thomas Jung and Michelle Moudy, and an amazing group of winners. We all went to Disney and we had a lot of fun.

The second time, I went to SAP TechEd in 2024 in West Palm Beach, FL. All the winners got to meet the SAP Developer Advocates: Rich, Josh, Thomas, Witaly, Nora and Michelle, and we spent a lot of time with all of them!! I was really happy to meet them and we all went together to Disney !!!

Also, I made a video sharing my career success with SAP you can check it out here: From a Failed Exam to a Successful Career: Sandra'... - SAP Community and I was on an episode of SAP Code talk podcast with Antonio Maradiaga!! SAP CodeTalk with Sandra Solis.

This is me so far, and I hope you're enjoying the blog! But stay with me, there's a few more below.

If you were 20 again, what would you study? 
A thousand times the same: Programming. But also, I’d focus on ABAP and dive into digital creation to enhance my video production.

What is your personal life dream?
My personal life dream is to achieve happiness and fulfillment. I want to create a balance between work and well-being. I aim to enjoy life and experience personal freedom, without being tied to conventional work norms. Above all, my dream is to continue growing emotionally and enjoy every moment of my life.

What is your professional life dream?
My professional life dream is to become the most recognized Latina, Mexican expert in the technical SAP field, contribute to the growth of the tech industry, and have the freedom to work creating impactful educational SAP content that will benefit the developer community. 

I also dream of encouraging people from Latam to explore all the SAP resources available to them, such as SAP Learning, developer tutorials, free trials, and more—many of which they might not even be aware of. I want to motivate them to participate in and attend SAP events like SAP TechEd, Devtoberfest contests, SAP Inside Tracks, and more. Additionally, I hope to inspire and encourage Latam professionals to stay active in the SAP Community and connect with the amazing Mentors, Champions, and Developer Advocates (little secret, I’m a big fan of). Can you believe that many people in Latam don’t even know about all of this?!

What do you most enjoy about the SAP Community?
All the resources they share like SAP learning, developer tutorials, the blogs and all answers the community provides. Also, all the events like SAP TechEd virtual and Devtoberfest - all of this is full of knowledge.

 

This is me so far, and I hope you have enjoyed!! 🥰

Thank you so much for taking the time to read my entire blog on SAP Community! I'm glad to hear that you found it useful/interesting. If you enjoyed it, I’d love for you to give it a like, leave a comment with your thoughts, or share it with others who might find it helpful. 

And now, I would like to Blog it Forward to my friend, and SAP Mentor: Raquel Cunha !! She is an active SAP Community contributor and passionate SAP Community.

entry.title: Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Coupa Adapter
entry.content:
Continuing my SAP CI Pagination blog series, this time with the Coupa Adapter! 

When integrating with Coupa, handling large datasets efficiently requires a proper pagination strategy. Unlike Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamics CRM, which rely on next page links, Coupa uses an offset-based pagination approach. Here, data is fetched in chunks based on the offset and limit values set in the adapter.

In this blog, I’ll cover:
✅How pagination works in the Coupa adapter using offset & limit
✅Best practices for optimizing API calls and handling large datasets
✅A step-by-step implementation to ensure seamless data retrieval

If you missed the last blogs in this series on Salesforce and Microsoft Dynamic CRM adapters, below are the links:

Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Salesforce Adapter

Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Microsoft Dynamics CRM Adapter 

Coupa Adapter - Handling Large Data Sets with Pagination

In this use case, we focus on retrieving records from the invoices resource in Coupa, which contains 50+ records. To achieve this, we use Query Resource operation to retrieve data. While the adapter provides an Auto Pagination feature that retrieves all pages in a single call, this approach can lead to significant performance issues when dealing with large datasets.

Fetching data page by page, processing each batch, and then moving to the next page is the best approach for handling large datasets. In this blog, I will explain the end-to-end batching process, ensuring seamless data synchronization while optimizing API performance.

Design Approach: 

The scenario involves sending invoices resource from Coupa to a target system that has a batch limit of 10 records per request. To accommodate this constraint, we implement a custom pagination approach using Coupa receiver adapter call.

The call fetches the page of records within a looping process to retrieve the pages one after the other. Each fetched page is passed through message mapping, ensuring seamless transformation before being delivered to the target system. This cycle continues—fetching a page, processing it, and sending it to the target—until all records are transferred, ensuring an efficient full end to end Batching processing and controlled data flow from Coupa to the target system.

Design components elaboration:

Timer : This is a timer based flow which be a scheduled as per the user's input

Content Modifier : "set properties" - This step is used to declare propeties such as CoupaResource, fieldnames, Coupa_limit, Coupa_offset, StartDate.

Property NameUse
CoupaResourceName of the resource that has to be queried
fieldnamesfieldnames of the resource that has to be queried
Coupa_limitNumber of records per page
Coupa_offsetoffset value, is set as "0" as we intend to fetch all the pages without missing a single record
StartDateDate from which the records to be queried, refers to updated-at in Coupa resource

Understanding Offset and Limit in Coupa Pagination

In this design, the two key parameters to control data retrieval are Offset and Limit. Unlike cursor-based pagination (as seen in Salesforce and MS Dynamics), Coupa follows an offset-based pagination approach, where:

Limit – Defines the number of records per page (e.g., if set to 10, each API call fetches 10 records).
Offset – Determines the starting point for the next batch of records. In our design after each request, the offset is incremented by the limit value (10) to fetch the next set of data.

  • The initial request starts with Offset = 0 and Limit = 10.
  • The next request will have Offset = 10, Limit = 10, fetching records from 11 to 20.
  • This process continues in a loop until the last page is reached.

Looping Process Call : "Initiate Coupa Call"

This Looping Process Call continuously runs until the  field is absent from the response payload. The condition for looping is: ${property.RecordsPresent} = 'true'

I have used a Groovy script to help us determine when to stop fetching records.

🔹After each Coupa API call, the response is checked for the following error:

<errors>
   <error>No results match your search criteria.</error>
</errors>

🔹If this error is found, it means there are no more pages left, and the script sets:
 RecordsPresent = false (End of pagination)

🔹Otherwise, if valid records are present, the script sets:
RecordsPresent = true (Continue fetching the next page)

The Looping Process Call continues until RecordsPresent = 'false', ensuring controlled pagination.

Coupa receiver adapter:

Theadapter is placed inside the Looping Process Call to fetch records page by page.

Processing Tab

-- Operation : Query Resource

-- Resource : Resource dynamically passed from ${property.CoupaResource}

-- Return Object Fields : Fields dynamically passed from ${property.fieldnames}

-- Limit : Defines the number of records per page (e.g., 10 records per page). This value cannot be passed dynamically and only accepts numeric values. To control it, we declare "Coupa.limit" as a property.

-- Offset : Determines the starting point for data retrieval in pagination. It must always start at 0 and cannot be passed dynamically, as it only accepts numeric values. To handle this limitation, we declare "Coupa.offset" as a property and increment its value after every API call to fetch the next set of records.

TheAdapter supports setting this via the properties "Coupa.limit"and "Coupa.offset" allowing better control over pagination.

We are not digging into the connection tab in this blog, this blog focuses only on pagination concept.

Groovy Script : Set Coupa Offset Parameter

Below script control the offset incrementation as well as last page identification

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def processData(Message message) {
    // Get the property value from the message and cast it to an integer
    def propertyValueString = message.getProperty("Coupa_offset")
    def propertyValue = propertyValueString ? propertyValueString.toInteger() : 0 // Convert to integer, default to 0 if null or empty
    def Coupa_limit = message.getProperty("Coupa_limit") as Integer
    
    // Add 50 to the Coupa_offset
    propertyValue += Coupa_limit

    // Set the modified offset value back to the message
    message.setProperty("Coupa_offset", propertyValue.toString()) // Convert back to string for setting property

    // Parse the body of the message
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String)
    def slurper = new groovy.util.XmlSlurper().parseText(body)

    // Check if the specific error message is present in the body
    def errorPresent = slurper.depthFirst().find { it.name() == 'error' && it.text() == 'No results match your search criteria.' } != null

    // Set the CountObjects property based on the presence of the error message
    message.setProperty("RecordsPresent", !errorPresent)

    return message
}

 

Router : "Data?" -

A router is used to determine whether the fetched page contains any records. If records are present, the integration flow proceeds to process to next steps. However, if the page is empty, the flow terminates immediately, preventing unnecessary processing.

This check ensures optimized execution by stopping the pagination loop when there are no more records to fetch, improving efficiency and reducing unnecessary API calls.

Process Call : "Message Mapping" - 

After fetching each page, the data is passed to Message Mapping, where it is processed before being sent to the target system. This step is target-specific and is not covered in this blog.

For demonstration purposes, I have included this step to mimic the "send to target" operation after message mapping. The key focus of this blog remains on pagination handling in the Coupa adapter.

Output  / offset and limit explaintion:

For a total of 54 records, the pagination process spans 6 pages:

  • First 5 pages contain 10 records each.
  • Page 6 contains the remaining 4 records.

When the 7th page is fetched, the response contains as below, which means end of records/pages.

<errors> <error>No results match your search criteria.</error> </errors>

Page 1 : Response from the Coupa system contains 10 records as per the set limit and with offset = 0 

Page 2 : Offset = Offset (10)+ limit(10) = 20, the adapter skips the first 10 records and responds with next 10 records in this call.

Page 3 : Offset = Offset (20)+ limit(10) = 30, the adapter skips the first 20 records and responds with next 10 records in this call.

Page 4 : Offset = Offset (30)+ limit(10) = 40, the adapter skips the first 30 records and responds with next 10 records in this call.

Page 5 : Offset = Offset (40)+ limit(10) = 50, the adapter skips the first 40 records and responds with next 10 records in this call.

Page 6 : Offset = Offset (50)+ limit(10) = 60, the adapter skips the first 50 records and responds with next 10 records in this call. This is the last page containing 4 records.

Page 7 : Offset = Offset (60)+ limit(10) = 70, the adapter skips the first 50 records and responds with next 10 records in this call. <error> tag in the response which means end of records/pages

Full run in TRACE

Wrapping Up

With limit and offset handling in place, we achieve a structured and efficient pagination mechanism in Coupa Adapter. By leveraging properties like Coupa.limit and Coupa.offset, along with a controlled looping process, we ensure seamless data retrieval without unnecessary API calls.

This approach not only optimizes performance but also ensures that large datasets are processed efficiently and accurately. Stay tuned for the next blog in this series, where we continue exploring pagination strategies across different adapters! 

I hope this helps!

Cheers,

Punith

entry.title: SAP e Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS: A Inovação Que Acelera o Futuro
entry.content:
🚀🏎️ SAP e Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS: A Inovação Que Acelera o Futuro – Agora no SAP Inside Track Porto!

Mais um ano, a SAP continua a impulsionar a performance da Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS dentro e fora das pistas! 🏁💨 Com tecnologia de ponta e dados em tempo real, a equipe consegue transformar precisão e inovação em vitórias. Mas e se essa mesma revolução tecnológica estivesse ao alcance do seu negócio?

📍 Bem-vindo ao Porto, cidade de inovação e tradição!
O Porto, com sua rica história de comércio e empreendedorismo, sempre esteve à frente do seu tempo. Desde os tempos dos navegadores que levaram inovação além-mar até os dias de hoje, com um ecossistema tecnológico vibrante, a cidade continua a liderar mudanças e a impulsionar negócios.

🖥️ E é exatamente isso que a SAP traz para as empresas de hoje:
✔ SAP HANA – O banco de dados revolucionário que processa grandes volumes de informação em tempo real.
✔ RISE e GROW with SAP – Modelos flexíveis de implementação ERP na nuvem, adaptáveis às necessidades de qualquer empresa.
✔ Inteligência Artificial integrada – Tornando a gestão empresarial mais intuitiva, eficiente e segura.

Agora, imagine tudo isso reunido em um evento imperdível! O SAP Inside Track Porto é o ponto de encontro para quem quer estar na vanguarda da tecnologia SAP.

📅 Data: 27-09-2025
📍 Local: The Lodge Hotel - Rua Serpa Pinto, 60 - 4400-307 V. N. Gaia – Porto

Confira as inscrições no Blog Grupo PORTO na SAP:
https://lnkd.in/d2Evvrnr


Inscreva uma palestra: https://lnkd.in/dYXjee2m
Venha ser sponsor: https://lnkd.in/dXPdvXDn
Venha ser voluntário : https://lnkd.in/dFyACjgG

Link para Tickets de participante:
https://lnkd.in/dqY8xG3b

Venha aprender, partilhar experiências e conectar-se com a comunidade SAP! 🚀

entry.title: SAP Builders, Welcome to the Application Development and Automation Group!
entry.content:
Big news, Builders! 

The SAP Builders group is now part of the Application Development and Automation group, bringing together even more application developers, automation enthusiasts, and low-code/no-code innovators into one thriving community. 

Why the Change? 

We’re combining forces to give you a stronger, more vibrant space for all things SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Code, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone, and beyond. This means more discussions, more resources, and a broader scope for your development journey. 

What Should You Do Now? 

  • If you were an SAP Builders group member, this is now your go-to place. 
  • Join the conversation! Ask questions, share insights, and keep the Builder mindset alive. 
  • Stay tuned for upcoming events, learning tips, and exclusive challenges designed just for you. 

This is just the beginning—we’re excited to continue growing and learning together! Let us know in the comments of this post what topics, resources, or activities you’d love to see in this newly expanded community. 

Welcome home, Builders!

entry.title: Enhancements for SAP Community - March 2025
entry.content:
Daylight Savings Time is here (for some of us)!

Spring forward with these enhancements and updates.

 

 

1) Updated Privacy Statement

We have updated our privacy statement.   The new version includes:

  • A description of how the SAP Consulting Capability processes published content
  • Addition of locations for the country and regional provision section

 Some users may be asked to accept these via the Legal Disclaimer dialog box.  This is normal whenever we make a change to our Privacy Statement and/or Terms of Service. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

2) Fully Expanded Editor Bar

To make editing your posts, comments, and replies easier, the editor bar will, by default, be fully expanded.

 

 

3) SAP Champion and Mentor Identifiers

Non-SAP employees Champions and Mentors will now have identifiers next to their profile names. 

      

 SAP employee champions/mentors already have the SAP logo - those employees signed in with their SAP accounts will see the SAP logo only as there cannot be two logos displayed side-by-side technically.

 

4) Event Reminder Timing

Reminders for events will now be sent 24hrs prior to the event start time. Previously the reminder was sent 30mins prior to the event taking place.

 

5) Board Selector Enhancements on Create Post

We have organized the drop-down list from which you select a board to place your post.  Previously the list displayed a selection of boards without organization or sequence.  

Now, the list is organized and sequenced.  

 

The user can also select Show All... to get to a more comprehensive and structured list.  (N.B. The list is a tree structure, so please drill down to the board on which you wish to post.)

 

6) Bug Fix: Source Code block adds blank lines

When creating a post which included code samples, extra white space around coding samples were added every time you enter-exit preview mode. These extra spaces were also added once the post was added. This inconsistency in formatting has been fixed.

Before:

     

After:

(No space before code block in preview mode or published.

     

 

7) Bug Fix: Format error when submitting a comment without text

Before (Error message displays vertically)

After (Error message displays horizontally, i.e., correctly)

entry.title: Experience at SAP CodeJam Kolkata 2025 || 4th March & 5th March, 2025
entry.content:
Reflecting on SAP CodeJam 2025 - Kolkata 


I’m thrilled to share my experience attending the SAP CodeJam 2025 in Kolkata, driven by SAP Developer advocates @noravonthenen , @Vitaliy-R , @sheenamk  and @eshrinivasan  hosted by Capgemini this year! The session centered around Machine Learning (ML) with Python and Generative AI (GenAI) in SAP, and it was an eye-opener into how these groundbreaking technologies are shaping the future of enterprise solutions.

Some key insights that stood out:

🤖 Empowering SAP Consultants with Real-Time AI: We discussed how GenAI and ML can help SAP consultants tackle real-time challenges. AI is enhancing efficiency and reducing the time spent on manual interventions. This is a game-changer in real-time project scenarios!

📊 Predictive Analytics for Smarter Decisions: With ML models embedded in SAP for smarter decision-making, such as optimizing processes, forecasting demand, and identifying risks—leading to more proactive, data-driven strategies.

⚡ Real-Time Problem Solving: GenAI tools are revolutionizing issue resolution during SAP implementations. By offering automated, instant troubleshooting, consultants can focus on high-value strategic decisions rather than spending time on repetitive system fixes.

🔧 Tailored Solutions at Speed: The ability of GenAI to generate custom code, configurations, and solutions in real-time allows SAP consultants to swiftly address client needs, significantly reducing implementation times while delivering highly personalized, impactful outcomes.

Here are some use cases and benefits of GenAI and ML with Python in SAP:

Use Cases:

  1. Predictive Analytics:

    • Use Case: Use ML models to forecast demand, optimize inventory, and predict financial trends in SAP.
    • Benefit: Helps businesses make data-driven decisions, reduce costs, and improve forecasting accuracy.

  2. Process Automation (RPA):

    • Use Case: Automate repetitive tasks like data entry, report generation, or invoice processing using Python scripts and GenAI.
    • Benefit: Reduces manual labor, minimizes errors, and accelerates process completion.

  3. Customer Insights and Personalization:

    • Use Case: Leverage ML to analyze customer behavior data and create personalized product recommendations in SAP.
    • Benefit: Improves customer satisfaction and drives sales by delivering more tailored experiences.

  4. Anomaly Detection:

    • Use Case: Detect outliers or unusual transactions in financial or inventory data using ML models trained in Python.
    • Benefit: Identifies potential fraud or errors quickly, enhancing security and data integrity.

  5. Automated Reporting and Dashboards:

    • Use Case: Use GenAI to generate custom reports and dashboards based on business parameters in SAP.
    • Benefit: Provides real-time insights without manual report creation, improving decision-making speed.

  6. Supply Chain Optimization:

    • Use Case: Use ML algorithms to optimize supply chain processes in SAP, from procurement to delivery.
    • Benefit: Reduces operational costs, minimizes delays, and enhances inventory management.

Benefits:

  1. Enhanced Efficiency:

    • Automates repetitive tasks, reducing manual effort and increasing overall system efficiency.

  2. Improved Accuracy:

    • ML models enhance decision-making accuracy by providing data-driven insights and predictions.

  3. Cost Savings:

    • Reduces costs by improving forecasting, streamlining processes, and identifying inefficiencies.

  4. Scalability:

    • Python and AI-powered models can scale to meet the growing data needs of businesses, ensuring systems can handle increased complexity over time.

  5. Faster Decision-Making:

    • Real-time insights from GenAI and ML lead to quicker and more informed decisions.

By integrating Python, GenAI, and ML into SAP, businesses can unlock automation, predictive capabilities, and smarter analytics, driving significant business value.

Apart from these, many key-points are taken away i.e. types of ML ( Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement Learning ), Training a data model, Business Data Cloud, Data Preparation, Feature Engineering, Data Frame, EDA etc. from @Vitaliy-R on 4th March, Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA. Again on 5th March, Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core session by @noravonthenen key-points ( AI Buzzwords )are taken away i.e. Fine Tuning, Orchestration including Grounding, Prompt Templating, Content Filter, Data Masking, LLM Access and all the hands-on procedure i.e. define Orchestration service deployment, AI model deployment etc.

Kudos to Chatrath Harinder Singh, @Sharmistha22 , Goutam Dasgupta, Abhishek Guha Roy for helping to make this event successful.

Also thank you @DataTherapist for believing in me to make this event a success. Also, thank you @Raya_16 and @TanushreeD  to make this event in Capgemini.

@ShilpaSAP @lauraschmitz @thomasjungblu @Abhishek_10 @KamallicaGhosh1 @Soham_SCM @SohamBhKoL 

Also sharing some of the glimpses of the SAP CodeJam Kolkata 2025.


#SAP #AI #MachineLearning #GenAI #FutureOfSAP #Capgemini #SAPCodeJam2025 #SAPRoadshow

entry.title: Reflections on SAP Code Jam Kolkata 2025 || 4th March & 5th March, 2025
entry.content:
Experience on SAP Code Jam 2025 - Kolkata

Thrilled to have attended SAP Code Jam 2025 in Kolkata, hosted by Capgemini this year. Big thanks to incredible SAP Developer Advocates @noravonthenen, @Vitaliy-R , @sheenamk and @eshrinivasan for driving such an insightful session.  The focus on Machine Learning (ML) with Python and Generative AI (GenAI) in SAP was truly an eye-opener. It’s exciting to see how these cutting-edge technologies are transforming the future of enterprise solutions!

Real-Time Scenario and Benefits of GenAI & ML with Python in SAP:

Scenario: Predictive Maintenance in Manufacturing

 

  • Situation: A manufacturing company uses SAP to manage its production line. Machines are integrated into SAP with real-time data being captured.

 

  • GenAI & ML Use: Machine Learning algorithms (implemented using Python) analyze historical data from equipment sensors (e.g., temperature, vibrations) to predict when a machine is likely to fail.

 

  • Outcome: Using GenAI models, SAP provides real-time alerts and recommendations for maintenance before a breakdown occurs, reducing downtime.

Benefits:

  1. Cost Savings: Reduces costly unplanned downtime and expensive repairs by predicting maintenance needs in advance.
  2. Efficiency Boost: Minimizes machine failure disruptions, ensuring smoother operations and higher productivity.

Data-Driven Decisions: SAP, powered by ML, uses real-time data to enhance decision-making, optimizing maintenance schedules and resource allocation.

Scenario: Sales Forecasting and Demand Planning

  • Situation: A retail company uses SAP for managing inventory and sales. They need to predict demand for products across different regions and seasons.
  • GenAI & ML Use: ML models built with Python analyze historical sales data to predict future product demand, factoring in trends, weather, holidays, and promotions.

Outcome: GenAI helps generate highly accurate sales forecasts, adjusting inventory levels proactively.

Benefits:

  1. Improved Forecast Accuracy: Generates more accurate sales forecasts, minimizing overstock or stockouts.
  2. Operational Efficiency: Optimizes inventory management, reducing storage costs and improving order fulfillment.
  3. Proactive Decision-Making: Allows business leaders to adjust strategies based on accurate, real-time demand predictions.

    In real-time, GenAI and ML with Python empower SAP systems to automate decision-making, forecast trends, and optimize business processes, driving efficiency and profitability.

     

    Kudos to Chatrath Harinder Singh,  @Sharmistha22 , Goutam Dasgupta, Abhishek Guha Roy for helping to make this event successful.

    Also thank you      for @DataTherapist believing in me to make this event a success. Also, thank you @Sayontan_Das  and @TanushreeD   to make this event in Capgemini.

     @ShilpaSAP @lauraschmitz @thomasjungblu @Abhishek_10 @KamallicaGhosh1 @Soham_SCM @SohamBhKoL 

    Also sharing some of the glimpses of the SAP CodeJam Kolkata 2025.

 

#SAP #AI #MachineLearning #GenAI #FutureOfSAP #Capgemini #SAPCodeJam2025 #SAPRoadshow

entry.title: ABAPConf 2025
entry.content:
Am 4. Juni 2025 ist ADventas einer der Gastgeber der ABAPConf 2025 in Hamburg!

Die Konferenz für den SAP Full Stack Entwickler - ABAP und Fiori. Hier teilen ABAP-Entwickler ihre Projekterfahrungen und stellen ihre Best Practices vor.

Seid mit vor Ort dabei und meldet euch über die Warteliste unter folgendem Link an: https://loom.ly/V9noCio

Informationen zur gesamten ABAPConf 2025 findet ihr hier: ABAPConf 2025

entry.title: 🎤 Palestras confirmadas para Recife, 10 de maio até o momento!
entry.content:
🎤 Palestras confirmadas para Recife, 10 de maio!
Garanta sua participação! pelo link: SAP Inside Track Recife 2025 #SITREC2025

💥 Seja um palestrante - Ainda dá tempo! 💥
Realize sua inscrição como palestrante e submeta seu tema para análise

entry.title: SAP CodeJam HANA ML in India 2025-03 Recap
entry.content:
At the beginning of March 2025, as part of the ML/AI Tour my fellow dev advocate @noravonthenen and I did in India, we held five SAP CodeJam events in Kolkata, Pune, and Bengaluru.

Two of these events focused on getting started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA and Python.

Equally important were chances to catch up with some organizers and participants in informal post-work settings...

...or with our SAP colleagues.

I am glad Nora and I could help so many participants to go through the journey of improving their skills working with ML and AI!

Traditionally, I very much enjoyed SAP Stammtisch at the end of the last CodeJam of the Tour.

#IamSAP 

I always take the opportunity to experience at least a little bit of the city I am in, so you can see my photos:

  • Kolkata: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG1wL0OydCp/ 
  • Pune: https://www.instagram.com/p/DG75rf0MPkt/ 

Now, the next stop for me is in Kraków on April 3rd.

Would you like to host such an SAP CodeJam? Here are the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/quot-getting-started-with-machine-learning-usin...
